title: 12 Assigning a Branch Capture Deposit and Managing Aged Old Deposits
body_text:
12	Assigning a Branch Capture Deposit and Managing Aged Old Deposits
12.1	Overview of Aged Open Deposits
Aged Open deposits are Branch Capture deposits that werenât transmitted to Central 1 within 24 hours of their creation and have remained in an open state. Examples of open states are: Open-Balanced, Open-Disapproved, Open-Incomplete, Open-Jammed, Open-Approved, or Open-Processing. An Aged Open Deposits tab displays on the top navigation menu for Supervisor Users when an Aged Open deposit is detected. See Section 9.14, Reviewing a Branch Capture Deposit, for deposit state descriptions. The tab doesnât display if there are no Aged Open deposits.
Supervisor Users Supervisor Users and/or Supervisor Users who are also assigned other Branch Capture user types (e.g., Capture User and/or Reviewer User) manage Aged Open deposits.
Aged Open deposits should be a rare occurrence, as deposits must be completed and transmitted the same day they are created, regardless of the Branch Capture deadline. The Branch Capture transmission deadlines are as follows:
5:30 pm local time for credit unions in Alberta and Saskatchewan; and
7:00 pm local time for credit unions in BC, Manitoba, Ontario, and the Atlantic region.
Important
:	Supervisors must monitor for the presence of an Aged Open Deposits tab daily. Aged Open deposits must be reviewed and processed immediately.
12.2	Managing Aged Open Deposits
Manage Aged Open Deposits as follows:
Log in to Branch Capture as described in Section 9.3, Logging in to Branch Capture.
Select
Aged Open Deposits
. See Figure 1.
Figure 1: Select âAged Open Depositsâ
Select the Edit icon on the appropriate deposit. See Figure 2.
Figure 2: Select Edit Icon
Assess the depositâs state to determine the appropriate action as described in the following table.
Important
:	Verify that items in the Aged Open deposit werenât included in another deposit before proceeding. If so, instruct the Capture User to delete the duplicate items and balance the deposit or delete the Aged Open deposit as appropriate. If the Capture User that created the deposit isnât available, assign the deposit to another Capture User. See Section 12.4, Assigning a Branch Capture Deposit, for procedures. Capture Users can edit or delete a deposit, provided that the deposit state isnât Open-Approved. Deposits with an Open-Approved deposit can only be transmitted for processing.
Deposit State(s)
Action Required
Open-Processing, Open-Incomplete, or Open-Jammed
If the deposit should be processed:
Have the Capture User who created or is assigned the deposit resolve outstanding errors and complete the deposit. See the related sections for procedures.
Have the appropriate user review and approve the completed deposit once itâs corrected. See Chapter 10, Disapproving or Approving a Branch Capture Deposit.
Once approved, transmit the deposit to Central 1 for processing. See Chapter 11, Transmitting a Branch Capture Deposit.
If the deposit shouldnât be processed:
Have the Capture User delete the deposit. See Section 9.13, Deleting a Branch Capture Deposit.
Open-Disapproved
If the deposit should be processed:
Have the Capture User who created or is assigned the deposit re-open the deposit, resolve outstanding errors, and complete the deposit. See the related sections for procedures.
Have the appropriate user review and approve the completed deposit once itâs corrected. See Chapter 10, Disapproving or Approving a Branch Capture Deposit.
Once approved, transmit the deposit to Central 1 for processing. See Chapter 11, Transmitting a Branch Capture Deposit.
If the deposit shouldnât be processed:
Have the Capture User delete the deposit. See Section 9.13, Deleting a Branch Capture Deposit.
Open-Balanced
If the deposit should be processed:
Have the appropriate user review the deposit. If they disapprove it, have the Capture User who created the deposit or is assigned the deposit re-open the deposit, resolve outstanding errors, and complete the deposit. See the related sections for procedures.
If they approve it, transmit the deposit to Central 1 for processing. See Chapter 11, Transmitting a Branch Capture Deposit.
If the deposit shouldnât be processed:
Have the Capture User delete the deposit. See Section 9.13, Deleting a Branch Capture Deposit.
Open-Approved
Transmit the deposit to Central 1 for processing. See Chapter 11, Transmitting a Branch Capture Deposit. Deposits in an Open-Approved state canât be edited or deleted (even if they contain errors).
12.3	Overview of Assigning a Branch Capture Deposit Deposits
Supervisor Users and/or Supervisor Users who are also assigned other Branch Capture user types (e.g., Capture User and/or Reviewer User) manage deposits in an editable state that arenât completed within normal time frames.
Example of editable states are: Open-Balanced, Open-Disapproved, Open-Incomplete, Open-Jammed, Open-Approved, or Open-Processing. âAged Open Depositsâ displays on the top navigation menu to Supervisor Users only if thereâs an Aged Open Deposit. See Section 9.14, Reviewing a Branch Capture Deposit, for deposit state descriptions.
This includes assigning a Branch Capture deposit to another Capture User to complete a deposit if the original Capture User isnât available to do so. (e.g., the original Capture User left for the day). Only Capture users who create or are assigned a deposit can edit and/or complete a deposit.
12.4	Assigning a Branch Capture Deposit
Assign a Branch Capture deposit to another Capture User as follows:
Log in to Branch Capture as described in Section 9.3, Logging in to Branch Capture.
Search for and open the appropriate deposit as described in Section 9.6, Searching for and Viewing a Branch Capture Deposit.
Select the Edit icon on the appropriate deposit. See Figure 1.
Figure 1: Select the Edit Icon
Select
Assign Deposit
. See Figure 2.
Figure 2: Select âAssign Depositâ
Select the appropriate Capture User from the Assign New User drop-down menu. If you have many users at the location, you must search for the appropriate user. Select
Find User
.
See Figure 3.
Figure 3: Searching for a User
Search for the appropriate Capture User on the User Search page as follows. See Figure 4.
â
Enter the userâs Client Centre ID (partial searches are allowed).
â¡
Select
Search
. Alternatively, select
View All
to view all users at the location.
â¢
Select the appropriate user from the List of Users.
Figure 4: Search for the Appropriate Capture User
Select
Save
to complete the assignment. See Figure 5. A confirmation message displays when the deposit is successfully assigned to the new Capture User. See Figure 6.
Figure 5: Select âSaveâ to Complete the Assignment
Figure 6: Successfully Assigned Deposit
END
